issues.title: Login redirect loops after session expiry
issues.summary: When a session expires mid-session, the redirect to /login bounces back to the original protected page, which redirects again. Missing return-URL guard.
issues.description:
Reproduces consistently when sitting on a protected route past the session TTL (currently 30m).

Steps to reproduce

  • Sign in.
  • Open /issues/142.
  • Wait 31 minutes (or invalidate the cookie in devtools).
  • Refresh.

Expected: sign-in page with a return URL; after auth, return to /issues/142.

Actual: sign-in → protected page → sign-in → … loop.
issues.error_message: Too many redirects detected on route /issues/142
issues.stack_trace:
at requireAuth (middleware/auth.ts:42)
at handleRedirect (router/index.ts:118)
… 12 frames omitted …
issues.expected_behavior: User lands on /login with a ?return= param; after successful auth they are sent back to the original route.
issues.actual_behavior: Redirect oscillates between /login and the protected route indefinitely.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Sign in.
2. Navigate to /issues/142.
3. Expire the session (wait 31m or clear the cookie).
4. Refresh the page.
issues.hypothesis: The auth middleware checks for the cookie before the router stores the return URL, so the return URL is always missing.

issues.title: OAuth callback hangs on slow networks
issues.summary: On 3G-throttled connections, the OAuth callback page never finalizes; spinner sits indefinitely with no error surfaced.
issues.description: Spinner appears, no timeout, no fallback. Observed consistently on Chrome with DevTools network throttled to 'Slow 3G'.
issues.error_message: No response from OAuth provider within expected window
issues.expected_behavior: A timeout of ~10s should surface a user-friendly error with a retry option.
issues.actual_behavior: Spinner runs indefinitely. No error, no fallback route.
issues.missing_information: Need to confirm whether the token exchange request is even being sent or if it stalls before that.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Throttle DevTools network to Slow 3G.
2. Click 'Sign in with GitHub'.
3. Approve the OAuth prompt.
4. Observe the callback page.
issues.hypothesis: The fetch to exchange the auth code has no AbortController / timeout set.

issues.title: Prompt editor loses cursor position on auto-save
issues.summary: Every time the auto-save fires (every 5s), the cursor jumps to the end of the textarea. Affects long prompts heavily.
issues.description: Auto-save calls setValue() on the CodeMirror instance, which resets internal selection state. Need to capture and restore the cursor range around the save.
issues.expected_behavior: Cursor stays in place after auto-save.
issues.actual_behavior: Cursor jumps to end of document on every save tick.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Open any prompt with >200 chars.
2. Place cursor in the middle.
3. Wait 5 seconds.
4. Observe cursor position.
issues.hypothesis: useAutoSave calls editor.setValue(content) without first capturing view.state.selection.

issues.title: Token usage counter resets mid-stream
issues.summary: The live token counter resets to 0 mid-generation on responses longer than ~800 tokens. Final count is correct but the UI flickers.
issues.description: Streaming token count in the sidebar resets mid-stream. Cosmetic only — the final token_usage stored is accurate — but confusing to users who are watching spend in real time.
issues.expected_behavior: Counter increments monotonically throughout the stream.
issues.actual_behavior: Counter resets to 0 around the 800-token mark, then climbs again.
issues.missing_information: Need to confirm if this only affects SSE or also fetch-based streaming.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Open /studio.
2. Submit a prompt that yields a long response (e.g. 'Write a 1000-word essay').
3. Watch the token counter in the sidebar.
issues.hypothesis: The streaming parser emits a new delta object on chunk boundaries which overwrites accumulated count instead of adding to it.

issues.title: Capstone submission PDF export truncates last page
issues.summary: PDF export via puppeteer cuts off the final ~40px of the last page. Happens when content height is not a clean multiple of A4.
issues.description: Affects the Report export route. All other export formats are fine. The truncation is consistent and reproducible with any report over 2 pages.
issues.error_message: Page content overflow detected on page 2+
issues.expected_behavior: All pages render completely without clipping.
issues.actual_behavior: Final page is clipped 40–60px from the bottom.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Create a report with more than 2 pages of content.
2. Click Export → PDF.
3. Open the downloaded PDF and scroll to the last page.
issues.hypothesis: Puppeteer's page height is calculated before fonts fully load, causing a slightly short viewport.

issues.title: Grading rubric weights don't sum-check on save
issues.summary: The rubric editor allows saving even if criteria weights don't total 100%. This leads to silent miscalculations in final scores.
issues.description: No client-side or server-side validation enforces that rubric weights sum to 100. A rubric with weights 30+30+30 = 90 saves and grades normally but produces wrong totals.
issues.error_message: Rubric weights do not sum to 100
issues.expected_behavior: Save is blocked and an inline error is shown when weights ≠ 100.
issues.actual_behavior: Save succeeds silently regardless of weight total.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Open any rubric in edit mode.
2. Set criteria weights to values that don't sum to 100.
3. Click Save.
4. Grade a submission using that rubric.
issues.hypothesis: The save handler was written before the weight-sum requirement was added to the spec.

issues.title: Peer review assignment algorithm ignores team membership
issues.summary: The random peer-review assignment sometimes pairs teammates, violating the project's conflict-of-interest rules.
issues.description: The assignment algorithm pulls from all enrolled users without filtering out members of the same team. Needs a pre-filter step.
issues.expected_behavior: No two reviewers from the same team are paired.
issues.actual_behavior: Teammates are occasionally assigned to review each other.
issues.missing_information: Need to clarify if TAs should also be excluded.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Create a course with at least two teams of 3.
2. Trigger peer review assignment.
3. Inspect assignment matrix for intra-team pairs.
issues.hypothesis: assign.ts queries users WHERE enrolled = true without joining team_members to exclude teammates.

issues.title: Quest progress resets on network reconnect
issues.summary: If the device goes offline and comes back, in-progress quest state reverts to the last server-persisted checkpoint, losing local steps.
issues.description: The offline-first sync merges by timestamp but doesn't prioritize local-latest over server-latest. A reconnect triggers a full re-fetch that overwrites the local state.
issues.error_message: Remote state timestamp is newer than local; overwriting.
issues.expected_behavior: Local progress is preserved and merged with server; user never loses steps taken while offline.
issues.actual_behavior: Quest progress rolls back to last server checkpoint on reconnect.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Start a quest and complete 2–3 steps.
2. Toggle airplane mode.
3. Complete another step.
4. Re-enable network.
5. Observe quest state.
issues.hypothesis: reconcile() uses server `updated_at` as the source of truth without a 'local-wins' flag for unsynced changes.

issues.title: Map markers cluster incorrectly below zoom level 10
issues.summary: At zoom levels below 10, nearby quest markers merge into a single cluster but the count badge shows 1 instead of the actual number.
issues.description: The clustering library is being called with maxZoom: 10 but the badge count delegate was accidentally wired to the first child's count rather than the cluster total.
issues.expected_behavior: Cluster badge shows correct count of all grouped markers.
issues.actual_behavior: Badge always shows '1' regardless of how many markers are clustered.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Open /map.
2. Zoom out past level 10 in an area with multiple nearby quests.
3. Observe the cluster badge.
issues.hypothesis: The createClusterCustomIcon function reads children[0].properties.count instead of cluster.getChildCount().

issues.title: Push notifications not delivered on iOS 17.4+
issues.summary: After Apple's iOS 17.4 WebPush changes, push notifications stopped arriving on Safari iOS. Android and desktop are unaffected.
issues.description: Apple introduced stricter VAPID key validation in 17.4. Our current VAPID key was generated with an outdated library version that produces keys Apple now rejects silently.
issues.error_message: DOMException: Registration failed - no active Service Worker
issues.expected_behavior: Push notifications are delivered on all platforms including iOS 17.4+.
issues.actual_behavior: Notifications silently fail on iOS 17.4+. No error shown to user.
issues.missing_information: Need to verify VAPID key format spec for WebKit 17.4.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Open app on iPhone running iOS 17.4+.
2. Enable notifications when prompted.
3. Trigger a push event from the backend.
4. Observe: notification never arrives.
issues.hypothesis: VAPID keys need to be regenerated using the web-push 3.x library with proper P-256 curve encoding.

issues.title: Thermostat schedule serialization drops timezone offset
issues.summary: When a thermostat schedule is saved, the timezone offset is stripped from the cron expression. Devices in non-UTC zones run schedules at the wrong time.
issues.description: The schedule builder calls toCron() which internally formats times in UTC without recording the original offset. The cron is stored and executed in UTC, not the device's local time.
issues.error_message: Timezone offset missing from cron expression: expected ±HH:MM suffix
issues.expected_behavior: Schedules fire at the intended local time regardless of the device's timezone.
issues.actual_behavior: Schedules fire at UTC equivalent times, which is wrong for non-UTC devices.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Set device timezone to America/Chicago.
2. Create a 7:00 AM heating schedule.
3. Observe device — it fires at 7:00 UTC (1:00 AM local).
issues.hypothesis: toCron() needs to accept and store a tzOffset param; the executor also needs a TZ-aware cron runner.

issues.title: Energy usage graph missing data for rooms with no events
issues.summary: Rooms that had zero activity in the selected period show no bar in the energy graph instead of a zero-height bar, making the room appear absent.
issues.description: The graph query only returns rows with events; it needs a LEFT JOIN or a fill-zero step so all rooms appear even with 0 usage.
issues.expected_behavior: All rooms appear in the graph; inactive rooms show 0 kWh.
issues.actual_behavior: Rooms with no events are absent from the graph entirely.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Add a room to a home but don't trigger any events.
2. Open the energy dashboard for the last 7 days.
3. Observe the graph — the room is missing.
issues.hypothesis: The SQL query uses INNER JOIN on events instead of LEFT JOIN, dropping rooms with no matching rows.

issues.title: MQTT reconnection floods broker with duplicate subscriptions
issues.summary: On reconnect, the MQTT client re-subscribes to all topics without first checking existing subscriptions, creating duplicates and flooding the broker.
issues.description: Each reconnect event calls subscribeAll() unconditionally. After 3 reconnects, the broker receives 3× the expected message volume per topic.
issues.error_message: Subscription already exists for topic hearth/+/sensor/temperature
issues.expected_behavior: Client checks existing subscriptions before re-subscribing; idempotent reconnect.
issues.actual_behavior: Duplicate subscriptions accumulate on each reconnect, causing message amplification.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Connect the MQTT client.
2. Force a disconnect (e.g. kill the broker briefly).
3. Allow reconnect.
4. Repeat 2–3 times.
5. Monitor the broker topic subscription list.
issues.hypothesis: subscribeAll() needs a guard that diffs the desired topic list against current subscriptions.
issues.resolution_notes: Added a Set-based subscription tracker in mqtt.ts. subscribeAll() now diffs against tracked subscriptions and only issues new SUBSCRIBE packets for missing topics. Tested across 10 forced reconnects — broker subscription count stays stable.

issues.title: Kernel panic on boot with >16 CPU cores detected
issues.summary: Dawn Kernel panics during early boot when the ACPI table reports more than 16 logical CPUs. Affects modern multi-core test machines.
issues.description: The CPU topology parser uses a fixed-size array of 16 entries. Any system reporting 17+ cores causes an out-of-bounds write in the BSP initialization path, triggering an immediate panic.
issues.error_message: PANIC: cpu_topology[16] out-of-bounds write at bsp_init+0x14a
issues.expected_behavior: Kernel boots successfully on systems with any number of logical CPUs.
issues.actual_behavior: Kernel panics immediately on boot when >16 CPUs are detected.
issues.missing_information: Need to confirm max CPU count from the ACPI spec to size the array correctly, or switch to dynamic allocation.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Boot Dawn Kernel on a machine with 17+ logical cores.
2. Observe panic in early boot output.
issues.hypothesis: cpu_topology is declared as cpu_topology[MAX_CPUS] with MAX_CPUS = 16 in smp.h. Needs to be dynamically allocated based on ACPI data, or MAX_CPUS increased to a safe upper bound (256).

issues.title: VFS read() returns stale data after write on tmpfs
issues.summary: A read() immediately following a write() on a tmpfs-mounted file returns the pre-write contents. The cache invalidation step is not running for tmpfs.
issues.description: tmpfs inodes are not being marked dirty after a write, so the page cache is not flushed before the next read. The issue does not reproduce on ext4.
issues.error_message: read() returned pre-write data; cache not invalidated
issues.expected_behavior: read() after write() returns the newly written data.
issues.actual_behavior: read() returns stale (pre-write) data from the page cache.
issues.missing_information: Need to confirm whether mark_inode_dirty() is being called at all in the tmpfs write path.
issues.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Mount a tmpfs volume.
2. Write 'hello' to a file.
3. Immediately read the file.
4. Observe stale contents.
issues.hypothesis: tmpfs_write_iter does not call mark_inode_dirty() or invalidate_inode_pages2() after completing the write.
